title: POV: You're the World's First Trillionaire (You Own $0)
hook: You cross one trillion dollars — the first human being who ever has — and in the same breath you learn you can't spend a cent of it, and the one call worth answering is already ringing in your pocket.
body: POV: you become the world's first trillionaire — and find out a trillion-dollar fortune is not money you can spend, but a cage you built so high you can't climb back out. You live it in second person, present tense, milestone by milestone, as the number climbs and everything you could actually keep falls to nothing: $0, a card declined at a gas station for a $4.50 coffee, one idea on a cracked laptop → $1 MILLION, on paper, a promise strangers made about your future → $100 MILLION, the IPO, where your worth is set every second by people you'll never meet → $1 BILLION, the deal that costs you the friend who built it with you → $100 BILLION, where a single sentence from you moves a currency → $1 TRILLION, the world's first, a number so large that a million dollars a day since the birth of Christ would still not have spent it → and the two levels above the money itself: YOU CAN'T SELL IT (sell a tenth and the whole number dies in your hand), and THE APPETITE — the hunger of a billion ordinary wants that priced you, that will price whoever comes next, and that never once needed your name. Real, verified wealth mechanics woven into the story: net worth is unrealized stock, not cash; the ultra-rich borrow against shares instead of selling; a trillion seconds is about 31,700 years; the five richest men doubled their fortunes while five billion people got poorer. You climb your whole life toward the center of the world and learn there is no center — only demand, breathing in the dark, hungry again by morning. Watch the whole series — every life, every level. Near-future dramatization for educational and entertainment purposes: no trillionaire exists yet; the milestone is a real projection (Oxfam estimates the first in the 2030s). Characters are fictional.
pinned: You end with the highest number a human has ever held and can't spend a cent of it — at which level would YOU have gotten off the ladder, if you'd known the number wanted you back?
poll: Where would you have stopped climbing? $1 million / $1 billion / $1 trillion / I'd never get off
